rulings.Case Title: LLOYD KENDRICK et al VS. BORGWARNER, INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Nissan North America, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain: 1) evidence that: Mrs. Kendrick was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant and 2) clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Regarding Mrs. Kendrick¿s exposure, Mr. Kendrick¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s responses to plaintiffs¿ special interrogatories from this case preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Regarding punitive damages, defendant¿s separate statement does not include any assertedly undisputed fact that defendant propounded contention discovery (other than requests for documents) regarding plaintiffs¿ request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LLOYD KENDRICK et al VS. BORGWARNER, INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Dana Companies, LLC¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and off calendar as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (breach of warranty, false representation and intentional tort). As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. As to issues 1-3, plaintiffs represent that they are dismissing those causes of action. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LLOYD KENDRICK et al VS. BORGWARNER, INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 6, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Toyota Motor Sales, USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (breach of warranty, false representation and intentional tort) and denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages). As to issues 1-3, plaintiffs represent that they are dismissing those causes of action. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Defendant¿s separate statement does not include any assertedly undisputed fact that defendant propounded contention discovery regarding plaintiffs¿ request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DOMINIC VENTIMIGLIA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday July 6, 2011, line 1. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment, or alternatively, motion for summary adjudication will be heard at 1:30pm in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are sustained only as to plaintiff¿s identification of defendant¿s work at the Stanford dorms. Its objections to all other portions of plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Kaiser Gypsum¿s interrogatory responses and the Federal Register excerpts are sustained. The court can take judicial notice of the Federal Register, but will not take judicial notice of the statements made in it to prove that the Kaiser Gypsum joint compound contained asbestos. If a hearing is requested, the prevailing party based on this tentative ruling must bring enough proposed orders to the hearing for endorsed copies. = (TLJ/503)

rulings.Case Title: DOMINIC VENTIMIGLIA VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday July 6, 2011, line 1. Defendant Douglass Insulation Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment will be heard at 1:30pm in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. As defendant demonstrated that plaintiff¿s discovery responses were verified, the court considered them. Plaintiff¿s discovery responses preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. If a hearing is requested, the prevailing party based on this tentative ruling must bring enough proposed orders to the hearing for endorsed copies. = (TLJ/503)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Carrier Corp.¿S Demurrer To Plaintiff¿S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday July 6, 2011, line 2. Defendant Carrier Corp.¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s first amended complaint will be heard at 1:30pm in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to plead facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc, 21 Ca.4th 71 (1999). If a hearing is requested, the prevailing party based on this tentative ruling must bring enough proposed orders to the hearing for endorsed copies. = (TLJ/503)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Carrier Corp.¿S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff¿S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Asbestos Law and Motion calendar for Wednesday July 6, 2011, line 2. Defendant Carrier Corp.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiff¿s first amended complaint will be heard at 1:30pm in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion to strike is off calendar as moot given the tentative ruling on defendant¿s demurrer. = (TLJ/503)
